US.tipping: 15-20% at restaurants, $1-2 per drink at bars, 15-20% for taxis
US.sim_advice: Buy prepaid SIM at any carrier store (T-Mobile, AT&T) or use eSIM (Airalo, Holafly)
US.water_safety: Tap water is safe to drink everywhere
US.timezone: Multiple zones: EST (UTC-5), CST (UTC-6), MST (UTC-7), PST (UTC-8)
US.currency_info: US Dollar (USD). Cards accepted everywhere. Carry some cash for tips.
US.dress_code: Casual. Some upscale restaurants require smart casual.

UK.tipping: 10-15% at restaurants (check if service charge included). Not expected at pubs.
UK.sim_advice: Buy SIM at Heathrow arrivals (Three, EE, Vodafone) or use eSIM
UK.water_safety: Tap water is safe to drink
UK.currency_info: British Pound (GBP). Contactless payments widely accepted.
UK.dress_code: Smart casual for most places. Some clubs enforce dress codes.

JP.tipping: No tipping — it can be considered rude
JP.power_plug: Type A, 100V 50/60Hz (same as US plugs, lower voltage)
JP.sim_advice: Rent pocket WiFi at airport or buy tourist SIM (Sakura Mobile, IIJmio)
JP.water_safety: Tap water is safe and excellent quality
JP.currency_info: Japanese Yen (JPY). Cash is king — many small shops don't take cards. Get yen at 7-Eleven ATMs.
JP.dress_code: Neat and modest. Remove shoes indoors. Cover shoulders at temples.

TH.tipping: Not expected but appreciated. Round up or leave 20-50 baht.
TH.sim_advice: Buy SIM at airport (AIS, DTAC, TrueMove). Very cheap data plans.
TH.water_safety: Do NOT drink tap water. Buy bottled water.
TH.currency_info: Thai Baht (THB). Cards accepted at malls/hotels. Street food is cash only.
TH.dress_code: Light, breathable clothes. Cover knees and shoulders at temples.

IN.tipping: 10% at restaurants. Round up for rickshaws/taxis.
IN.sim_advice: Buy SIM at airport (Jio, Airtel). Need passport copy. Activation takes 1-24 hours.
IN.water_safety: Do NOT drink tap water. Use bottled or filtered water only.
IN.currency_info: Indian Rupee (INR). UPI payments very common. Cards accepted at most places in cities.
IN.dress_code: Modest at religious sites. Light cotton clothes for heat.

AE.tipping: 10-15% at restaurants. Service charge often included.
AE.sim_advice: Buy SIM at airport (du, Etisalat). Tourist SIMs available.
AE.water_safety: Tap water is safe but most people drink bottled
AE.currency_info: UAE Dirham (AED). Cards accepted everywhere. ATMs widely available.
AE.dress_code: Modest in public — cover shoulders and knees. More relaxed in hotels/resorts.

SG.language: English, Mandarin, Malay, Tamil (English is primary)
SG.tipping: Not expected — service charge usually included
SG.sim_advice: Buy tourist SIM at Changi Airport (Singtel, StarHub)
SG.water_safety: Tap water is safe and excellent quality
SG.currency_info: Singapore Dollar (SGD). Cashless society — cards and PayNow everywhere.
SG.dress_code: Casual. Air conditioning is intense — bring a light layer.

FR.tipping: Service included by law. Round up for good service.
FR.sim_advice: Buy SIM at any Tabac shop or Orange/SFR store
FR.water_safety: Tap water is safe to drink
FR.currency_info: Euro (EUR). Cards widely accepted. Some markets are cash-only.
FR.dress_code: Smart casual. Parisians dress well — avoid overly casual/touristy outfits.

emergency_numbers.note: Look up local emergency numbers before your trip

_default.tipping: Varies by country — research local customs
_default.sim_advice: Buy a local SIM at the airport or use an eSIM service like Airalo
_default.water_safety: When in doubt, drink bottled water
_default.currency_info: Check exchange rates before travel. Notify your bank of travel plans.
_default.dress_code: Research local dress codes, especially for religious sites
